title: Save The Last Trance For Me

jokes.text: I want something simple. Just me and my boys barking like seals at naked women. [BARKING]
jokes.setup: Will wants a simple bachelor party
jokes.punchline: His idea of simple is literally barking like seals at strippers
jokes.explanation: Strong character moment - Will's straightforward honesty about wanting something crude is both endearing and funny. The actual barking sound effect sells it.

jokes.text: But, Will, that's so politically incorrect. No one likes naked women anymore, believe me.
jokes.setup: Carlton objects to Will's strip club idea
jokes.punchline: His reasoning is hilariously wrong and the 'believe me' suggests personal experience
jokes.explanation: Perfect Carlton - earnest, wrong, and accidentally revealing. The 'believe me' is the perfect character touch.

jokes.text: Tell me again, why did I make you my best man? You said I'm like the brother you never had. No, no, I said you like the brother I never wanted.
jokes.setup: Carlton misremembers Will's reason for making him best man
jokes.punchline: Will's brutal correction reveals his true feelings
jokes.explanation: Classic Will/Carlton dynamic. The misdirection is well-structured and the punchline lands hard. Very quotable.

jokes.text: When you wake up, you will be very, very short. Damn, see, that stuff does work.
jokes.setup: Will mockingly tries to hypnotize Carlton about height
jokes.punchline: Will acts like it worked because Carlton is already short
jokes.explanation: Simple height joke exploiting Carlton's stature. Functional but predictable.

jokes.text: [IN NATIVE AMERICAN ACCENT] Distant drums say man with giant pants... forget important interview.
jokes.setup: Uncle Phil forgot Nicky's school interview
jokes.punchline: Will's mock-mystical commentary on Phil's size and forgetfulness
jokes.explanation: Classic Will deflection through humor. The accent and mystical framing is creative, though the fat joke is familiar territory.

jokes.text: Well, can't you cancel it? [LAUGHING] Good one, Daddy. Ha.
jokes.setup: Phil asks Hilary to cancel her hair appointment
jokes.punchline: She treats it like he told a hilarious joke
jokes.explanation: Perfect Hilary moment - her priorities are so warped that canceling a hair appointment seems impossible. Great character integration.

jokes.text: Certainly, sir. I'll just have to make this triple fudge bundt cake tomorrow.
jokes.setup: Geoffrey is asked to drive Nicky instead of his planned activity
jokes.punchline: His 'sacrifice' is making dessert tomorrow instead of today
jokes.explanation: Classic Geoffrey - treating his luxurious butler duties as hardships with perfect deadpan delivery.

jokes.text: Sure, my bike has a basket.
jokes.setup: Ashley is asked if she can drive Nicky to his interview
jokes.punchline: She offers to take him on her bicycle
jokes.explanation: Perfectly economical joke. Ashley's matter-of-fact delivery makes the absurd solution seem reasonable.

jokes.text: Or that weird dude that be vacuuming our pool.
jokes.setup: Will suggests alternatives to drive Nicky
jokes.punchline: Casually mentions their pool cleaner as an option
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Will's casual attitude toward their wealth and staff.

jokes.text: Say, don't you have a class now? Oh, that's not important.
jokes.setup: Will claims education is very important to him
jokes.punchline: Immediately dismisses his own class as unimportant
jokes.explanation: Perfect hypocrisy reveal. The immediate contradiction shows Will's true priorities despite his claims.

jokes.text: Or you could say, 'In two years, I'll be bigger than you.'
jokes.setup: Carlton coaches Nicky with a proper interview answer
jokes.punchline: Will offers a threatening alternative
jokes.explanation: Great subversion of interview advice. Will's street logic applied to kindergarten admissions is perfect character work.

jokes.text: Well, you need to stop pressuring him. You treat him like a ventriloquist's dummy. That's not what I'm doing. Nicky, what school do you wanna go to? The best school in L.A. Crescent Rock School. Yeah, I was wrong, Carlton. You're the dummy.
jokes.setup: Will accuses Carlton of treating Nicky like a ventriloquist's dummy
jokes.punchline: Carlton immediately proves the point, leading to Will's perfect comeback
jokes.explanation: Excellent joke construction. The setup creates expectation, Carlton immediately validates Will's criticism, and the punchline perfectly caps it. The dummy/dummy wordplay is satisfying.

jokes.text: Now I know what Uncle Phil must feel like in a Miata.
jokes.setup: Will is cramped in a small play area
jokes.punchline: Compares his situation to Uncle Phil in a tiny car
jokes.explanation: Good callback to Uncle Phil's size. The comparison works well and shows Will thinking of family even in awkward moments.

jokes.text: Hi, I'm Nicky. Is there any apple juice?
jokes.setup: Formal introduction at prestigious school
jokes.punchline: Nicky immediately asks about juice like a regular kid
jokes.explanation: Sweet character moment showing Nicky as authentically childlike despite the formal setting. Good contrast with the pretentious environment.

jokes.text: That may be why the other adults were standing.
jokes.setup: Will introduces himself while apparently sitting in a child-sized chair
jokes.punchline: Ruby politely points out why adults don't sit there
jokes.explanation: Polite way of pointing out Will's physical comedy situation. Works as setup for the visual gag.

jokes.text: You know, he just wrote, produced, directed his first screenplay. The Forest Gumby.
jokes.setup: Will tries to match another parent's bragging about their child
jokes.punchline: Makes up an absurd movie title combining Forrest Gump and Gumby
jokes.explanation: Creative wordplay that captures Will's improvised lying. The combination is unexpected and memorable.

jokes.text: Look, I can make myself dizzy. That's a part of his comedy act. Yeah, that one killed them at Def Jam, Junior.
jokes.setup: Nicky spins around making himself dizzy
jokes.punchline: Will desperately tries to spin it as professional comedy performance
jokes.explanation: Good cringe comedy as Will desperately tries to save face. 'Def Jam Junior' is a nice touch showing Will's background.

jokes.text: Oh, hey, well, Nicky here is a big interacter. You might call him the last interaction hero, you know.
jokes.setup: Teacher mentions observing how kids interact
jokes.punchline: Will turns 'interact' into 'Last Action Hero' reference
jokes.explanation: Decent wordplay showing Will's pop culture mind, though the pun is a bit forced. Shows him trying too hard to impress.

jokes.text: This is that mountain from Hawaii, Mount Kichikichiyayadada.
jokes.setup: Nicky's clay sculpture looks like a rock
jokes.punchline: Will makes up an elaborate fake Hawaiian mountain name
jokes.explanation: Will's desperation leads to creative nonsense. The fake name is funny but not exceptional. Good character moment of improvised panic.

jokes.text: It's really a turtle. He's inside his shell. Is he hiding because he's nervous about going to a new school? No, he's watching Power Rangers.
jokes.setup: Teacher tries to find deep meaning in Nicky's turtle sculpture
jokes.punchline: Nicky's innocent, literal answer about TV
jokes.explanation: Excellent misdirection. Teacher expects psychological depth, gets kids' TV show reference. Perfect childhood authenticity vs adult projection.

jokes.text: What, an artificial heart valve?
jokes.setup: Will looks at another child's sophisticated clay creation
jokes.punchline: Assumes it's advanced medical equipment
jokes.explanation: Good escalation of the overachieving kids theme. The specific medical reference is unexpected and funny.

jokes.text: Oh, great. You know, I mean, not 'great' great, you know. But it was good. It was good. You know. You know, not that good, you know. But... What is the word I'm looking for? Bad? Worse than that, but I'll go with bad.
jokes.setup: Carlton asks how the interview went
jokes.punchline: Will's elaborate verbal gymnastics to avoid saying it was terrible
jokes.explanation: Good escalating comedy as Will tries to soften bad news. A bit wordy but the building panic is funny.

jokes.text: Will's the one that got yelled at for stealing. Well, she had it coming after the way she talked about your mountain. It was a turtle.
jokes.setup: Nicky reveals Will got in trouble for stealing
jokes.punchline: Will's defensive justification, followed by Nicky's correction
jokes.explanation: Good callback to earlier scene. Will's protective instincts gone wrong, followed by Nicky's innocent correction.

jokes.text: Are you sure that while Aunt Viv was pregnant with Carlton... she wasn't, you know?
jokes.setup: Carlton excitedly talks about the hypnotist
jokes.punchline: Will implies Viv might have drunk during pregnancy to explain Carlton's behavior
jokes.explanation: Dark humor that pushes boundaries for family TV. The implication without stating it directly shows good writing restraint, though it's edgy material.

jokes.text: Tippy The Drunken Penguin is his opening act. Ooh, you think we could get him for my bachelor party?
jokes.setup: Carlton reads about the hypnotist show
jokes.punchline: Gets excited about a drunken penguin act for Will's bachelor party
jokes.explanation: Perfect Carlton moment - his wholesome weirdness thinking a drunken penguin is appropriate bachelor party entertainment. Very quotable name.

jokes.text: Oh, come on, Uncle Phil, hasn't there been enough black-on-black violence?
jokes.setup: Uncle Phil threatens to kill Will
jokes.punchline: Will frames family discipline as social commentary
jokes.explanation: Excellent meta-humor that acknowledges real social issues while deflecting family tension. Shows Will's street-smart perspective and social awareness in a comedic context.

jokes.text: Look, Will, she thinks she's naked. Isn't this exciting?
jokes.setup: Woman on stage thinks she's naked due to hypnosis
jokes.punchline: Carlton's innocent excitement about the inappropriate situation
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Carlton's naive enthusiasm even in awkward situations. His innocence makes the cringe funny rather than uncomfortable.

jokes.text: Ooh. Shoot me, oh, Great Mentos, shoot me.
jokes.setup: Will doesn't want to be hypnotized
jokes.punchline: Sarcastically asks to be shot instead
jokes.explanation: Standard reluctant volunteer humor. Will's sarcasm is in character but not particularly original.

jokes.text: Pie. Oh, my God, Uncle Phil.
jokes.setup: Carlton mentions Will hasn't touched his pie
jokes.punchline: Will suddenly realizes they're late because of Uncle Phil's threat
jokes.explanation: Good story callback showing Will's delayed reaction to their lateness. Works to move plot forward.

jokes.text: Could I have my coat? My fault.
jokes.setup: Will is hypnotized to act like a 4-year-old
jokes.punchline: Politely asks for coat like a small child would
jokes.explanation: Good physical comedy setup. Will Smith's adult body with child mannerisms would be visually funny. Simple but effective.

jokes.text: I've replaced all the video tapes with insect documentaries.
jokes.setup: Family preparing for school interview by hiding inappropriate items
jokes.punchline: Ashley's contribution is replacing videos with nature docs
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Ashley's intelligence and the family's attempt to seem refined. Specific detail about insects is nicely absurd.

jokes.text: It's their end-of-the-season thong blowout. Everything in there is half off. You know what I'm saying?
jokes.setup: Hilary reading Victoria's Secret catalog
jokes.punchline: Will's double entendre about 'half off'
jokes.explanation: Classic double entendre playing on sale prices vs. clothing coverage. Predictable but well-executed for the setup.

jokes.text: Aren't you glad I use Dial?
jokes.setup: Will comments that something smells good
jokes.punchline: Geoffrey references the Dial soap commercial tagline
jokes.explanation: Good commercial reference that fits Geoffrey's character. The misdirection from food to personal hygiene is well-timed.

jokes.text: I seen Uncle Phil pick bigger chunks of food out of his teeth.
jokes.setup: Geoffrey's crab puffs are small
jokes.punchline: Will compares them to Uncle Phil's dental hygiene discoveries
jokes.explanation: Gross but funny image that ties to Uncle Phil's established eating habits. Good specificity with 'chunks' rather than generic food particles.

jokes.text: That never works.
jokes.setup: Uncle Phil tells someone to come in without opening the door
jokes.punchline: Will points out the obvious flaw in the logic
jokes.explanation: Simple observational humor pointing out sitcom logic. Very economical and relatable, though not groundbreaking.

jokes.text: [IN CHILD'S VOICE] I gotta go potty. Hey, the buddy system stops at the pool, mister.
jokes.setup: Hypnotized Will acts like a 4-year-old needing bathroom help
jokes.punchline: Carlton refuses to help with bathroom needs
jokes.explanation: Good escalation of the hypnosis situation. Carlton's boundary-setting is funny and the 'buddy system' reference is clever.

jokes.text: It must be all that rap music.
jokes.setup: Carlton notices Will acting weird
jokes.punchline: Blames it on rap music like a conservative parent
jokes.explanation: Perfect Carlton response - blaming social problems on music. Shows his conservative mindset and disconnect from Will's culture.

jokes.text: Can he make me think I care?
jokes.setup: Carlton explains Will has been hypnotized
jokes.punchline: Geoffrey's disinterested response
jokes.explanation: Perfect Geoffrey response - dry, dismissive, and economically delivered. Shows his bemused tolerance of family drama.

jokes.text: Look at that, the boy would rather read than walk. Isn't that beautiful?
jokes.setup: Nicky can't stand because his legs are numb
jokes.punchline: Phil spins it as intellectual dedication
jokes.explanation: Good parental spin control. Phil's desperate reframing of a medical issue as an intellectual virtue is both funny and relatable.

jokes.text: But this is a bass. Well, the cello's in the shop, this is a loaner.
jokes.setup: Hilary claims to play cello while holding a bass
jokes.punchline: Her absurd explanation for the wrong instrument
jokes.explanation: Perfect Hilary logic - confidently wrong and making up elaborate explanations. The 'loaner' concept for instruments is absurd.

jokes.text: Actually, madam, I got it from the Gary Coleman Cookbook. Hope you like short ribs.
jokes.setup: Vivian lies about getting recipe from fancy French bistro
jokes.punchline: Geoffrey reveals it's from Gary Coleman cookbook with height pun
jokes.explanation: Excellent multi-layered joke. Gary Coleman reference, height pun with 'short ribs', and Geoffrey undermining Vivian's pretensions. All elements work together perfectly.

jokes.text: You like seafood? Of course. See, food.
jokes.setup: Child-Will asks if visitor likes seafood
jokes.punchline: Shows food in his mouth for the classic kid joke
jokes.explanation: Classic kid joke that works because of the hypnosis context. Low originality but perfect character integration for a 4-year-old mentality.

jokes.text: I'll give you a snickerdoodle. Okay.
jokes.setup: Carlton needs to get child-Will to come with him
jokes.punchline: Bribes him with cookies like an actual child
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Carlton adapting to treat Will like an actual child. Simple but effective.

jokes.text: Why, certainly, Master Carlton. I just happen to have a trolley in my trousers.
jokes.setup: Carlton asks if Geoffrey has anything with bells
jokes.punchline: Geoffrey's sarcastic response about having a trolley in his pants
jokes.explanation: Classic Geoffrey sarcasm with absurd specificity. The trolley image is unexpected and the alliteration makes it memorable.

jokes.text: Simon says, 'Freeze.'
jokes.setup: Carlton tells child-Will not to move
jokes.punchline: Will uses Simon Says rules to justify movement
jokes.explanation: Clever use of children's game rules. Shows Will's hypnotized mind still working with kid logic to find loopholes.

jokes.text: Okay. What's the most musical part of your body? I don't know. Your nose because you can pick it and blow it.
jokes.setup: Child-Will asks a riddle about musical body parts
jokes.punchline: Answer plays on musical terms and bodily functions
jokes.explanation: Classic kid joke with decent wordplay. Low originality but perfect for the hypnotized child character.

jokes.text: Excuse me, mister, I'll shine your head for a nickel.
jokes.setup: Child-Will continues inappropriate behavior during interview
jokes.punchline: Offers to shine the man's bald head for money
jokes.explanation: Good escalation of inappropriate child behavior. The specific price and service offer make it funnier than a generic insult.

jokes.text: For the love of God, does nobody have a bell?
jokes.setup: Family situation spiraling out of control
jokes.punchline: Carlton's desperate plea for the one simple solution
jokes.explanation: Good escalation of desperation. The religious plea adds urgency to the absurd situation.

jokes.text: What the hell is in those crab puffs?
jokes.setup: Will snaps out of hypnosis
jokes.punchline: Assumes the food caused his weird behavior
jokes.explanation: Good callback that shows Will's confusion about his lost time. Logical assumption given the context.

jokes.text: Too bad we got stuck behind that ice cream truck on the way there. Yeah, those bells turned you into a raving maniac.
jokes.setup: Carlton explains Will's behavior
jokes.punchline: Doesn't realize he's explaining the real cause
jokes.explanation: Good ironic humor as Carlton unknowingly explains the real cause. The audience gets the joke even if the characters don't.

jokes.text: They said it was his sincerity that impressed them the most. They liked that he was just being himself. That's what Will told me to do.
jokes.setup: Nicky got accepted to the good school
jokes.punchline: His authentic behavior was what impressed them, validating Will's advice
jokes.explanation: Nice callback that validates Will's original advice. Shows the lesson that authenticity beats pretense.

jokes.text: You know, it just seems to me... that there might be a cash gift involved here somewhere, I don't know. Well, I'll settle for just a hearty handshake.
jokes.setup: Will helped Nicky succeed
jokes.punchline: Tries to negotiate payment then settles for gratitude
jokes.explanation: Classic Will trying to monetize good deeds. The quick backtrack is in character but not exceptional.

jokes.text: [DOORBELL RINGS] [BARKING]
jokes.setup: Doorbell rings again
jokes.punchline: Will starts barking like he mentioned wanting for his bachelor party
jokes.explanation: Excellent callback to the very first joke. The doorbell triggers the barking, suggesting ongoing hypnotic effects. Great episode bookend.

jokes.text: Look, I'll walk him, but somebody else gonna have to pooper scoop.
jokes.setup: Will continues barking like a dog
jokes.punchline: Others treat him like an actual dog needing walks
jokes.explanation: Good escalation of the dog joke with practical considerations. The specificity of 'pooper scoop' makes it funnier.

tmdb_overview: Put into a trance by a stage hypnotist, Will behaves like a 4-year-old just as the family gears up for an interview with the admissions head of a private school.
